channel.title: Business - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.description:
1. Bitcoin prices news: BTC heads back to $77,000 on Middle East peace deal  CoinDesk
  2. Bitcoin slips as U.S.-Iran peace deal hopes offset by crypto ETF outflows  Investing.com
  3. Major news emerged from the Strait of Hormuz, driving a broad rally across cryptocurrency markets! Bitcoin briefly rebounded above $77,000.  富途牛牛
  4. Iran-US ceasefire extension signals diplomatic progress, markets shift to risk-on mode  Crypto Briefing
  5. Crypto Market Rally Sparks As Crude Oil Plunges Amid US-Iran Deal Hopes  Benzinga

item.description:
1. Family demands answers after eldery woman dies following altercation at Tim Hortons  TheGrio

item.title: Nissan unit scraps plan to make EV powertrains in UK, Nikkei says - Reuters
item.description:

item.title: Nvidia CEO Urges Super Micro to Tighten Up Amid Taiwan Crackdown - Bloomberg.com
item.description:
1. Nvidia CEO Urges Super Micro to Tighten Up Amid Taiwan Crackdown  Bloomberg.com
  2. After $2.5 billion Supermicro smuggling bust, Nvidia CEO urges company to fix export control compliance — Taiwan also begins to crack down on AI GPU chip smuggling to China  Tom's Hardware
  3. Taiwan investigates three for alleged illegal export of high-end AI servers  Reuters
  4. Three detained over alleged smuggling of Nvidia chips to Hong Kong  Focus Taiwan
  5. Taiwan raids 12 locations in its first formal crackdown on Nvidia AI chip smuggling — hunts three fugitives for document forgery, fraudulent declarations in Super Micro smuggling case  Yahoo Finance

item.title: ‘No nonsense’ Wall Street titans playing nice with Mamdani are part of the problem - New York Post

item.title: Packagist Supply Chain Attack Infects 8 Packages Using GitHub-Hosted Linux Malware - The Hacker News
item.description:
1. Packagist Supply Chain Attack Infects 8 Packages Using GitHub-Hosted Linux Malware  The Hacker News
